    CAGEMATCH Â» Events Database Â» WWE Friday Night SmackDown #1209

    WWE Friday Night SmackDown #1209

    TV-Show

    • Results
    • Card
    • Card with guide
    • Champions
    • Additional events
    • Ratings
    • Comments
    Name of the event:
    WWE Friday Night SmackDown #1209
    Date:
    21.10.2022
    Promotion:
    World Wrestling Entertainment
    Type:
    TV-Show
    Location:
    Toledo, Ohio, USA
    Arena:
    Huntington Center
    Broadcast type:
    Live
    Broadcast date:
    21.10.2022
    TV station/network:
    FOX
    Commentary by:
    Michael Cole & Wade Barrett
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 7.02 based on 100 votes. ::::
    Singles Match
    Solo Sikoa (w/Jey Uso, Jimmy Uso & Sami Zayn) defeats Sheamus (w/Butch & Ridge Holland) (13:05)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 5.49 based on 73 votes. ::::
    Singles Match
    Liv Morgan vs. Sonya Deville - Double Count Out (8:50)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 6.67 based on 91 votes. ::::
    WWE Women's Tag Team Title Match
    Damage CTRL (Dakota Kai & IYO SKY) (w/Bayley) (c) defeat Raquel Rodriguez & Shotzi (9:58)
    :::: Matchguide Rating: 7.19 based on 93 votes. ::::
    Singles Match
    Rey Mysterio defeats Ludwig Kaiser (w/Giovanni Vinci & Gunther) (11:25)
    :::: Not eligible for the matchguide: dark matches cannot be recommended. ::::
    Dark Match
    Matt Riddle defeats The Miz
    :::: Not eligible for the matchguide: dark matches cannot be recommended. ::::
    Dark Non Title Match
    Ronda Rousey defeats Natalya
    All workers
    Bayley, Butch, Dakota Kai, Damage CTRL, Giovanni Vinci, Gunther, IYO SKY, Jey Uso, Jimmy Uso, Liv Morgan, Ludwig Kaiser, Matt Riddle, Natalya, Raquel Rodriguez, Rey Mysterio, Ridge Holland, Ronda Rousey, Sami Zayn, Sheamus, Shotzi, Solo Sikoa, Sonya Deville, The Miz

    Your Options:
    • Read latest comments
    • Read all comments
    Other:
    • Details & History
    • Ratings System Rules
    • Help and formula
    Latest comments of the CAGEMATCH inmates
    Smi-48 wrote on 03.03.2026:
    [5.0] "Sikoa has a challenging task with Sheamus, but one that he can also win. However, they don't leave it at that, and the Bloodline attacks the Irishman's arm with chair shots. Wyatt's promo is recorded today and once again cryptic. Meanwhile, Morgan continues to show her fierce side and attacks Deville viciously even after the match is over. It seems as if the Raiders are being rebooted, at least according to the following vignette. Then Strowman and Omos make a match for Crown Jewel. It's certainly the smarter decision to give Damage CTRL the win as a stable. Rey is set up for Gunther by being allowed to take out his minions. And while Logan Paul gives a clean-cut promo, Jey Uso breaks the OTC rules. We'll probably find out next week if there will be any consequences."
    Garmonbozia wrote on 22.10.2025:
    [6.0] "This was a solid episode. The opener and the last match were solid. The last segment was pretty cool."
    PavelDragunov wrote on 02.11.2022:
    [7.0] "Logan Paul repeated the well-known proverb, came, talked, knocked out. Sami and Uso are already the most interesting thing on the show according to the classics, from beginning to end they know how to create content. Omos pushing Strowman is interesting and now I'm waiting for their match clearly more out of curiosity about whether they can make a real show. Bray Wyatt still seems to be waiting for something. Brawling Brutes hope to find something to answer Uso and Sami."
    flightaker321 wrote on 24.10.2022:
    [7.0] "The Bray Wyatt stuff to me is just weird and I'll never truly grasp onto him like others. The opening match was good, the post match angle was strong also, the women's tag and so was Rey vs Kaiser. Liv turning into Sabu because she had one hardcore match is funny. Logan Paul's promo had good delivery, but nobody buys him as a babyface so it doesn't even matter. It'll be interesting to see how Roman reacts to Jey not obeying his orders next week. Overall a good show this week"
    fgpfriends wrote on 23.10.2022:
    [8.0] "Very good episode of Smackdown which I liked, Triple H is on fire and every show under his control was atleast 6/10 for me, today one is a very big 8, almost 8.5, I liked this show, lot of stuff happend, Logan Paul and Bray gave amazing speeches, the matches were good at least, lovable episode!"
    Bammer21 wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [7.0] "A very entertaining smackdown, Brays promo was very interesting as was the promo from whoever is tormenting him. Braun and Omos had a fairly standard staredown segment and Anything with the bloodline is gold."
    eltetechori wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [8.0] "A very good show, pretty solid and with good stories. Although I noticed the absence of wrestlers like Drew, Ricochet, etc, I thought it was a great show."
    raininglightning wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [6.0] "This show was kinda just there for me. None of the matches stood out, with Sikoa/Sheamus and Liv/Sonya ruined by their endings. No great storyline progression this week and a few of the matches dragged. I'm finding the Triple H era shows to be an improvement, but still ultimately unremarkable."
    BuffKitty93 wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [6.0] "The show was kinda in the middle for me. The best match was definitely Sheamus vs. Solo Sikoa and the Bray stuff is definitely interesting. I can't wait to see where it goes."
    Delyosis wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [9.0] "Great show. I liked the opener and the main event. Good progress in the storylines. Very intrigued and excited with the Bray Wyatt story. Very good smackdown."
    BUIOOMEGA wrote on 22.10.2022:
    [9.0] "The show is super cool even if it's not a masterpiece of an event, but of course, it's a very good one."
